relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: An heir can still live like an enslaved child before maturity.
Guardians hold the child until the appointed time.
Elemental powers once held the immature world.
In fullness of time, the Son comes from woman and under Law.
Release comes so adoption can be received.
The Spirit of the Son cries Abba in the heart.
No longer enslaved, the child becomes heir through the Divine.
Before knowing the Divine, they served what were not gods.
Being known by the Divine makes return to weak elemental slavery tragic.
Days, months, seasons, and years become signs of returning bondage.
Paul fears his labor may become empty.
He begs them to become as he is, as he became as they are.
His first good-news visit came through bodily weakness.
They did not despise his bodily testing but received him as Divine messenger.
Their former blessedness was so generous they would have given their eyes.
Truth-telling now risks being treated as enmity.
False zeal tries to shut them out so they become dependent.
Beautiful zeal stays beautiful even when Paul is absent.
Paul labors in birth until Messiah is formed in them.
He longs to be present and change tone because he is perplexed.
Those wanting to be under Law are asked to actually hear the Law.
Abraham’s two sons become the allegorical field.
Flesh-birth and promise-birth are contrasted.
Paul reads the women as two covenantal modes.
The Sinai/Hagar image names slavery in the allegory.
The Jerusalem above is free and motherly.
The barren one is called to rejoice in unexpected abundance.
The siblings are children of promise like Isaac.
Flesh-pattern resists Spirit-birth.
The inheritance belongs to freedom, not slavery.
The community is called children of the free woman.

contemplative_note: Galatians 4 is birth-labor for freedom. Abba Spirit, Messiah formed within, and promise-mother imagery all press the community away from returning to slavery-patterns.
